translation: By the fig and the olive,
And by this city (Makkah) of peace,

arabic: لَقَدۡ خَلَقۡنَا ٱلۡإِنسَٰنَ فِيٓ أَحۡسَنِ تَقۡوِيمٖ
transliteration: Laqad khalaqna alinsana fee ahsani taqweemin
translation: Surely, We created the human in the best of forms!
Then We reverted the human to the lowest of the low,
notes: In the light of Verse 4 above, a human being may be considered as the best of Allah’s creation. Apparently, everything around him is made subservient to man’s needs. And he is expected to believe in Allah and do righteous deeds as indicated in Verse 6 below. But by failing to do that, he virtually disowns the ‘best’ attribute applied to his own creation. Allah Almighty then reverts his status to that of his lowly beginning and lowers it further below that of other animals.

arabic: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ فَلَهُمۡ أَجۡرٌ غَيۡرُ مَمۡنُونٖ
transliteration: Illa allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati falahum ajrun ghayru mamnoonin
translation: Except for those who believed and did righteous deeds. For them, an unending reward!
What, then, do they deny you [O Prophet] for, post this Judgment [in Verses 5 and 6 above]?
Is not Allah the Best of all judges?
